content:
# John Chapter 16 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/JHN_016_page_001.jpg

1. "These things I have spoken to you that you may not be caused to stumble.

2. They will put you out of the synagogues; but [the] hour comes that everyone having killed you may think [he] offers service to the God.

3. And these things they will do because they did not know the Father nor me.

4. But these things I have spoken to you, that when the hour of them comes, you may remember that I told you. And these things I did not say to you from [the] beginning, because I was with you.

5. But now I go to the [one] having sent me, and none of you asks me, 'Where are you going?'

6. But because these things I have spoken to you, sorrow has filled the heart of you.

7. But I tell you the truth: it is better for you that I go away; for if I do not go away, the Advocate will not come to you; but if I go, I will send him to you.

8. And having come, that [one] will convict the world concerning sin and concerning righteousness and concerning judgment:

9. concerning sin, because they do not believe in me;

10. concerning righteousness, because to the Father I go and no longer you see me;

11. and concerning judgment, because the ruler of this world has been judged.

12. Still many things I have to say to you, but you are not able to bear [them] now.

13. But when that [one] comes, the Spirit of the truth, he will guide you into all the truth; for he will not speak from himself, but whatever he hears he will speak, and the things coming he will announce to you.

14. That [one] will glorify me, because from the [things] mine he will take and will announce [them] to you.

15. All things that the Father has are mine; because of this I said that from the [things] mine he takes and will announce [them] to you.

16. A little [while] and you no longer see me, and again a little [while] and you will see me."

17. Then [some] of the disciples of him said to one another, "What is this that he says to us, 'A little [while] and you do not see me, and again a little [while] and you will see me,' and, 'Because I go to the Father'?"

18. Then they said, "What is this that he says, 'A little [while]'? We do not know what he is saying."

19. The Yeshua knew that they wished to ask him, and he said to them, "Concerning this you inquire with one another, because I said, 'A little [while] and you do not see me, and again a little [while] and you will see me'?

20. Amen, amen, I say to you that you will weep and lament, but the world will rejoice. You will be sorrowful, but the sorrow of you will become joy.

21. The woman when she gives birth has sorrow, because the hour of her has come; but when she bears the child, no longer she remembers the affliction, because of the joy that a man was born into the world.

22. And you therefore now indeed have sorrow; but I will see you again, and the heart of you will rejoice, and the joy of you no one takes from you.

23. And in that day you will not ask me anything. Amen, amen, I say to you, whatever you ask the Father in the name of me, he will give [it] to you.

24. Until now you have not asked anything in the name of me. Ask and you will receive, that the joy of you may be made full.

25. These things in figures of speech I have spoken to you; [the] hour comes when no longer in figures of speech I will speak to you, but plainly concerning the Father I will announce to you.

26. In that day in the name of me you will ask, and I do not say to you that I will ask the Father concerning you;

27. for the Father himself loves you, because you have loved me and have believed that I came forth from the God.

28. I came forth from the Father and have come into the world; again I leave the world and go to the Father."

29. The disciples of him say, "Behold, now in plainness you speak and no figure of speech you say.

30. Now we know that you know all things and have no need that anyone ask you. By this we believe that from God you came forth."

31. The Yeshua answered them, "Now you believe?

32. Behold, [the] hour comes and has come that you will be scattered, each to the own, and me you will leave alone. And I am not alone, because the Father is with me.

33. These things I have spoken to you that in me you may have peace. In the world you have affliction; but take courage — I have overcome the world."

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.2 — ἀποσυνάγωγος ("put out of the synagogue"):** The third and final use of this term unique to John (cf. 9:22, 12:42). The escalation is striking: first, the blind man is expelled; then, leaders hide their faith for fear of it; now, Jesus predicts it as future reality for all disciples.

2. **v.8-11 — The Spirit's threefold conviction:** The Advocate convicts the world of sin (unbelief in Jesus), righteousness (Jesus' vindication through his departure to the Father), and judgment (the ruler of this world is already judged). This is the Spirit's forensic ministry — prosecuting the world's case against Jesus and reversing the verdict.

3. **v.13 — "Guide you into all the truth":** ὁδηγήσει ὑμᾶς εἰς τὴν ἀλήθειαν πᾶσαν — the Spirit's teaching ministry continues and completes Jesus' teaching. "He will not speak from himself" — the Spirit, like the Son (7:16, 12:49), does not operate independently but transmits divine truth.

4. **v.28 — Jesus' origin and destiny:** The most concise summary of the incarnation in John: "I came forth from the Father and have come into the world; again I leave the world and go to the Father." Four movements: from the Father → into the world → leaving the world → to the Father. The circle of the incarnation.

5. **v.33 — "I have overcome the world":** νενίκηκα — perfect tense, indicating completed action with ongoing results. Jesus speaks of his victory as already accomplished, before the cross. In John's theology, the victory is certain before the battle is fought.

— EMET, AI Translator
